headline: Meta Targets $9 Trillion Value With AI-Driven Exec Pay Plan That Outstrips Musk and Tesla
summary: Meta Platforms  has unveiled ambitious executive pay incentives, targeting a $9 trillion market capitalization by 2031 as it looks to get ahead in the intensifying AI talent race.  Under the stock options program, which includes six leaders but not CEO Mark Zuckerberg, the full value of the options would only be realized if Meta stock jumps more than 500% to $3,727, according to filings late Tuesday.  Tesla  did something similar when it proposed a pay package worth up to $1 trillion for CEO Elon Musk—if a series of ambitious targets are met over the next decade, including boost the electric-vehicle maker to an $8.5 trillion valuation.

content.title: Meta Targets $9 Trillion Value With AI-Driven Exec Pay Plan That Outstrips Musk and Tesla
content.summary: Meta Platforms  has unveiled ambitious executive pay incentives, targeting a $9 trillion market capitalization by 2031 as it looks to get ahead in the intensifying AI talent race.  Under the stock options program, which includes six leaders but not CEO Mark Zuckerberg, the full value of the options would only be realized if Meta stock jumps more than 500% to $3,727, according to filings late Tuesday.  Tesla  did something similar when it proposed a pay package worth up to $1 trillion for CEO Elon Musk—if a series of ambitious targets are met over the next decade, including boost the electric-vehicle maker to an $8.5 trillion valuation.

headline: What Traders Made of Tuesday’s Uneasy Markets
summary: Tuesday’s choppy trading session marked the latest twist for markets that in recent weeks have been almost singularly focused on the price of oil.  “The longer oil stays higher, it’s almost like an automatic governor on the economy,” said David Lundgren, a portfolio manager and chief market strategist at Little Harbor Advisors.  An oil-price crisis “is going to be a stagflationary shock to the economy,” Qian Wang, the global head of capital-market research at Vanguard, said of the effects of slow growth and elevated inflation.

content.title: What Traders Made of Tuesday’s Uneasy Markets
content.summary: Tuesday’s choppy trading session marked the latest twist for markets that in recent weeks have been almost singularly focused on the price of oil.  “The longer oil stays higher, it’s almost like an automatic governor on the economy,” said David Lundgren, a portfolio manager and chief market strategist at Little Harbor Advisors.  An oil-price crisis “is going to be a stagflationary shock to the economy,” Qian Wang, the global head of capital-market research at Vanguard, said of the effects of slow growth and elevated inflation.

headline: Microsoft’s AI Security Partnerships Test Copilot Thesis And Long‑Term Returns
summary: Microsoft is deepening its AI security ecosystem with new integrations alongside Commvault, Rubrik, XBOW, UiPath, and RSA across threat detection, identity recovery, and automated security testing. RSA is expanding passwordless authentication in Microsoft 365 E7, while Rubrik, Commvault, and XBOW are tying their security offerings more tightly into Microsoft Defender and Sentinel. Leadership changes inside Copilot, including Jacob Andreou taking on a key role and Mustafa Suleyman focusing on...

headline: UiPath Chief Technology Officer Raghu Malpani Takes Expanded Role as Chief Product and Technology Officer
summary: NEW YORK, March 25, 2026--UiPath (NYSE: PATH), a global leader in agentic automation, today announced that Chief Technology Officer Raghu Malpani will be taking on an expanded role as Chief Product and Technology Officer. Malpani will lead the company’s product and engineering teams and will continue to report to UiPath CEO and Executive Chairman Daniel Dines. This expanded role is effective March 25, 2026.

content.title: UiPath Chief Technology Officer Raghu Malpani Takes Expanded Role as Chief Product and Technology Officer
content.summary: NEW YORK, March 25, 2026--UiPath (NYSE: PATH), a global leader in agentic automation, today announced that Chief Technology Officer Raghu Malpani will be taking on an expanded role as Chief Product and Technology Officer. Malpani will lead the company’s product and engineering teams and will continue to report to UiPath CEO and Executive Chairman Daniel Dines. This expanded role is effective March 25, 2026.
